debug_info.goal:
A : Type u_1
inst✝⁸ : CommRing A
inst✝⁷ : IsNoetherianRing A
inst✝⁶ : @HeightOneLocalizationIsPID A inst✝⁸
M : Type u_2
inst✝⁵ : AddCommGroup M
inst✝⁴ : Module A M
inst✝³ : Module.Finite A M
hMT : IsTorsion A M
N : Type u_3
inst✝² : AddCommGroup N
inst✝¹ : Module A N
inst✝ : Module.Finite A N
hNT : IsTorsion A N
⊢ M ∼ₚᵢₛ[A] N ↔
    ∀ (p : PrimeSpectrum A),
      p.asIdeal.primeHeight = 1 →
        Nonempty
          (LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl M ≃ₗ[Localization p.asIdeal.primeCompl]
            LocalizedModule p.asIdeal.primeCompl N)

failed_attempts:
constructor
  · intro ⟨f, hf⟩ p hp
    exact ⟨LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ (hf.bijective_of_primeHeight_le_one (le_of_eq hp))⟩
  · intro H
    -- Use isPseudoIsomorphism_iff_bijective_map: it suffices to find f : M → N
    -- such that S⁻¹f is bijective, where S is the combined support submonoid.
    -- First, construct a combined localization equivalence S⁻¹M ≅ S⁻¹N.
    set sigma := (Module.support A M ∪ Module.support A N) ∩
      {p : PrimeSpectrum A | p.1.primeHeight = 1}
    set S := ⨅ p ∈ sigma, p.1.primeCompl
    -- Construct the combined localization equivalence S⁻¹M ≅ S⁻¹N.
    -- Since both M and N are torsion, their localizations at height 1 primes
    -- determine the combined localization (by subsingleton_convert).
    -- The iso at each height 1 prime assembles into an iso of the combined localization.
    have hequiv : Nonempty (LocalizedModule S M ≃ₗ[Localization S] LocalizedModule S N) := by
      sorry
    -- Lift the combined localization equivalence to a global map.
    obtain ⟨e⟩ := hequiv
    obtain ⟨f, hf_bij⟩ := exists_map_bijective_map_of_equiv_localizedModule S e
    -- Show f is a pseudo-isomorphism.
    exact ⟨f, (LinearMap.isPseudoIsomorphism_iff_bijective_map hMT hNT f).mpr hf_bij⟩
